stem: A GPS navigation navigation system uses 4 parallel satellite links for redundancy. The position fixed requires at least one satellite link to function. satellite link 1 has a reliability of 82%. satellite link 2 has a reliability of 91%. satellite link 3 has a reliability of 88%. satellite link 4 has a reliability between 75% and 92% (uncertain due to limited operational data). Additionally, there is a common-cause failure probability (e.g., power outage, shared software bug) that could disable all satellite links simultaneously, estimated between 4.3% and 8.0%. What is the overall probability that the position fixed?

ground_truth.answer: P(position fixed) ranges from 0.9195 to 0.9568. Point estimate: 0.9387.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 4 satellite links fail simultaneously).
With common-cause failure (4.3%-8.0%): P(works) = (1-P_cc) × P(independent works). Range: [0.9195, 0.9568].
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
Ignoring common-cause failures that can disable all paths simultaneously
